description: Figure 25 presents simulated evolutions of microstructure and dislocation density distribution on the YZ surface of low-alloy steel; the caption reports a temperature of 950 °C and that panel (a) shows a transiently decreasing strain rate from 0.1 s⁻¹ to 0.01 s⁻¹ at 0.4 true strain, whereas panel (b) shows a constant strain rate of 0.01 s⁻¹ at 0.42 true strain. The left column of (a) displays microstructure maps at 0.4, 0.42, and 0.45 strain, with a visible legend indicating white grains as matrix, green grains as first-stage dynamically recrystallized (DRX) grains, and red grains as second-stage DRX grains. The right column provides corresponding dislocation density distributions with a visible color scale spanning 0.01 to 8 × 10¹³ m⁻². At 0.4 strain, image labels indicate a first-stage DRX fraction of 42.9% and a dislocation density range of 1 × 10¹¹–3.689 × 10¹⁴ m⁻². By 0.42 strain, first-stage DRX is 84.8% with total DRX at 85.8%, and the maximum dislocation density drops to 1.133 × 10¹⁴ m⁻². At 0.45 strain, first-stage DRX reaches 93.3% and total DRX reaches 96.7%, with dislocation density up to 7.167 × 10¹³ m⁻². In (b), the microstructure exhibits a DRX fraction of 83.0% and the dislocation density ranges up to 5.169 × 10¹³ m⁻². These maps demonstrate that a transient strain rate reduction promotes rapid DRX growth and significant dislocation density reduction, with second-stage grains marking multi-stage recrystallization, supporting the core conclusion that transient deformation conditions strongly influence DRX kinetics and spatial heterogeneity.
